args.explanation: Reading all core files of the Nepal Van Java tourism website to understand the full structure, design, and content of the landing page and its related assets.

args.response:
<result>
  <reasoning>All 10 requested files were read in a single batch. The project is a static tourism website for "Nepal van Java" (Dusun Butuh, Magelang) built with Vite + Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS. The landing page has 4 main sections: Cinematic Hero, Sticky Story, Floating Bento Destinations, and Impact/CTA. Design uses a Santorini-inspired navy-blue palette (#0E2F76 primary, #F5FEFF background, #AAC0E1 accent) with Plus Jakarta Sans font, glassmorphism navbar/footer, scroll animations, and a bilingual (ID/EN) language switcher.</reasoning>
  <search_summary>
    <found>
      - index.html: Full landing page with 4 sections (Hero, Story, Bento grid, CTA/Stats) + inline navbar and footer
      - base.css: CSS variables (Santorini palette), typography, reset. Font: Plus Jakarta Sans. Colors: --bg-main #F5FEFF, --text-primary #0E2F76, --bg-accent #AAC0E1
      - layout.css: Glassmorphism floating navbar (fixed, rounded, backdrop-blur), 3-column footer grid, mobile nav drawer, full responsive breakpoints
      - pages.css: All section styles — cinematic hero (100vh, hero-bg.jpg, gradient overlay), sticky story (2-col grid), bento grid (3-col asymmetric cards with glass hover overlay), impact CTA (dark navy gradient), plus styles for all other pages (destinasi, profil, kuliner, homestay, galeri, kontak)
      - main.js: Scroll header effect, mobile nav toggle, ID/EN translation dictionary (full), parallax hero, IntersectionObserver scroll animations
      - components/navbar.html: Draft navbar with rolling-text hover animation on all 7 nav links, flag-based language switcher
      - components/footer.html: 3-column footer (brand, quick links, contact+social)
      - components/card-wisata.html: Tourism card component with image, price badge, title, desc, location, link
      - package.json: Vite 5.2.11, no other dependencies
      - project-context.md: Full design system rules — Santorini palette, anti-template UX rules, directory structure, mobile-first mandate
    </found>
    <not_found>
      - wa-handler.js and detail-router.js (not requested, but exist in assets/js/)
      - Actual hero-bg.jpg, home-bg-blur.jpg, profil-bg.jpg local image content (binary files)
      - vite.config.js multi-page config (not requested)
    </not_found>
  </search_summary>
  <relevant_files>
    <file>index.html</file>
    <file>assets/css/pages.css</file>
    <file>assets/css/layout.css</file>
    <file>assets/css/base.css</file>
    <file>assets/js/main.js</file>
    <file>components/navbar.html</file>
    <file>components/footer.html</file>
    <file>components/card-wisata.html</file>
    <file>package.json</file>
    <file>project-context.md</file>
  </relevant_files>
  <relevant_ranges>
    <file path="index.html">
      <range start="1" end="220"/>
    </file>
    <file path="assets/css/pages.css">
      <range start="1" end="350"/>

  <relevant_file_ranking>
    <ranked_file rank="1">
      <path>index.html</path>
      <reason>The main landing page — contains full structure: navbar, hero, story, bento destinations, impact/CTA stats, footer. All 4 content sections are here with bilingual data-lang-key attributes.</reason>
    </ranked_file>
    <ranked_file rank="2">
      <path>assets/css/pages.css</path>
      <reason>Primary section styling file — contains all visual styles for the landing page sections (cinematic hero, sticky story, bento grid, impact CTA) plus all subpage styles. Largest and most detailed CSS file.</reason>
    </ranked_file>
    <ranked_file rank="3">
      <path>assets/css/layout.css</path>
      <reason>Defines the glassmorphism navbar and footer layout, responsive breakpoints, mobile nav drawer, and rolling-text hover animation states.</reason>

      <reason>Core design tokens — all CSS custom properties (colors, spacing, shadows, transitions, radii), typography defaults, CSS reset. Foundation for entire design system.</reason>
    </ranked_file>
    <ranked_file rank="5">
      <path>assets/js/main.js</path>
      <reason>All interactive behavior — scroll header effect, mobile nav, full ID/EN translation dictionary, hero parallax, and IntersectionObserver scroll-reveal animations.</reason>
    </ranked_file>
    <ranked_file rank="6">

      <reason>Design system documentation — Santorini palette rules, anti-template UX constraints, typography hierarchy, directory structure, and prompting workflow the project was built against.</reason>
    </ranked_file>

      <reason>Shows the minimal tech stack: Vite 5.2.11 only, no CSS frameworks, no component libraries — pure vanilla approach.</reason>
    </ranked_file>
  </relevant_file_ranking>
</result>

## Summary for Redesign

Here's what you need to know about the current state:

**Design System:**
- Font: Plus Jakarta Sans (Google Fonts)
- Palette: `#F5FEFF` (bg), `#0E2F76` (navy primary), `#AAC0E1` (blue-grey accent), `#FFFFFF` (secondary)
- Tech: Vite + Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS, no frameworks

**Landing Page Sections (top to bottom):**
1. **Cinematic Hero** — 100vh, local `hero-bg.jpg`, dark gradient overlay, centered title/subtitle/tagline, scroll indicator, parallax on scroll
2. **Sticky Story** — 2-column grid (text left, sticky portrait image right), 3 descriptive paragraphs about the village
3. **Floating Bento** — Asymmetric 3-column grid of destination cards (large + medium + tall), glass hover overlay reveals title/desc/link
4. **Impact CTA** — Dark navy gradient section, 3 stat counters (15+ spots, 100+ homestays, 0 pollution), CTA heading + button

**Navbar:** Fixed floating pill (glassmorphism, rounded-20px), rolling-text hover animation on all links, ID/EN flag switcher, scrolled state changes text color from white to navy

**Footer:** 3-column glassmorphism (brand | quick links | contact+social), rounded top corners (30px), WhatsApp + Instagram + Facebook + TikTok icons

**Interactivity:** Scroll animations (IntersectionObserver fade-up), parallax hero content, mobile drawer nav, full bilingual translation dictionary in main.js
